Title: Rosmarinic Acid Synthase 1 Phosphorylation by SmMAPK3 Is Required for Salicylic Acid-Induced Salvianolic Acid Accumulation in Salvia miltiorrhiza Hairy Roots

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Rosmarinic acid/salvianolic acid branch linking phenylpropanoid and tyrosine-derived pathways
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Rosmarinic acid-related intermediate leading to rosmarinic acid and salvianolic acid B
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as a key enzyme directing the phenylpropanoid pathway and the tyrosine pathway toward salvianolic acid biosynthesis; required for accumulation of rosmarinic acid and salvianolic acid B
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for target-product formation; key enzyme/branch switch toward salvianolic acids
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key enzyme and switch in salvianolic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Rosmarinic acid synthase; BAHD acyltransferase-type synthase inferred from enzyme name
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Main active medicinal components with clinical value in treatment of myocardial ischemia and hypoxia
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases rosmarinic acid and salvianolic acid B; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout sharply decreases both; phosphomimic S178D further enhances accumulation whereas nonphosphorylatable S178A abolishes this enhancement
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmRAS1 is a core biosynthetic enzyme required for salvianolic acid accumulation in S. miltiorrhiza hairy roots. 2. SmMAPK3 directly interacts with and phosphorylates SmRAS1 at Ser178. 3. Ser178 phosphorylation increases SmRAS1 protein stability and promotes rosmarinic acid and salvianolic acid B accumulation, especially under salicylic acid treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; phosphosite mutagenesis (S178A/S178D); HPLC metabolite profiling; Y2H; pull-down; CoIP; BiFC; LCI; MST; in vitro and in vivo phosphorylation assays; protein stability assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 50 μM salicylic acid in S. miltiorrhiza hairy roots; 1.50 mM salicylic acid in N. benthamiana transient assays; MG132 in degradation assay
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious penalty reported for SmRAS1 overexpression or phosphomimic in hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for metabolic engineering to increase salvianolic acid production; stacking with SmMAPK3 is more effective than single-gene overexpression; phosphomimic engineering at Ser178 may further enhance yields
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion states SmRAS1 functions as the first step directing phenylpropanoid and tyrosine pathways toward salvianolic acids. SmRAS1S178D increased RA and SalB more than SmRAS1 overexpression, while SmRAS1S178A had no significant effect.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein phosphorylation activation/stabilization of target enzyme
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Post-translational control of salvianolic acid biosynthesis through phosphorylation and stabilization of SmRAS1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Phosphorylates SmRAS1 at Ser178, enhancing its stability and thereby promoting salvianolic acid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential mediator of salicylic acid-induced salvianolic acid accumulation; kinase activity is required
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Main active medicinal components with clinical value in treatment of myocardial ischemia and hypoxia
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases rosmarinic acid and salvianolic acid B and is required for salicylic acid-induced accumulation; RNAi suppresses both and blocks salicylic acid response; kinase-dead/autophosphorylation-defective K70R fails to support induction
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmMAPK3 is essential for salicylic acid-induced rosmarinic acid and salvianolic acid B accumulation. 2. SmMAPK3 directly binds SmRAS1 and phosphorylates it at Ser178. 3. Salicylic acid enhances SmMAPK3 phosphorylation, SmMAPK3-SmRAS1 interaction, SmRAS1 phosphorylation, and SmRAS1 stability, and coexpression with SmRAS1 strongly boosts salvianolic acids.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hairy-root overexpression and RNAi; constitutively active and kinase-defective mutant analysis; SA treatment; HPLC metabolite profiling; Y2H; pull-down; CoIP; BiFC; LCI; MST; in vitro kinase assay; in vivo Phos-tag assays; protein stability assays; double-transgenic coexpression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salicylic acid treatment (50 μM in hairy roots; up to 1.50 mM in N. benthamiana transient assays)
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Phosphoproteomics mentioned in Discussion using empty vector and SmMAPK3-RNAi lines; accession not provided
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive activation (CA-SmMAPK3) caused hypersensitive response, premature senescence of hairy roots, growth inhibition, strong reduction of rosmarinic acid and salvianolic acid B, and increased tanshinones; continuous MAPK activation limited experiments
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful engineering target for boosting salvianolic acids, especially combined with SmRAS1, but constitutive activation is risky; strategies should preserve inducible/controlled kinase activity rather than continuous activation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CA-SmMAPK3 showed robust autophosphorylation but continuous activation reduced target metabolites due to likely HR/stress costs. Discussion also notes interactions with SmKSL1 and SmCYP71D375 in tanshinone pathway, but these were not primary nutrient-metabolism core genes in Results.
